generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components (inferred from data sources)
instance IoTSensorDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregates contextual data traces from heterogeneous physical sources including IoT sensors, PLCs, and ERP systems for Digital Twin integration. Supports event-triggered and periodic data collection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData IoTSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance PLCDataCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects operational parameter data from Programmable Logic Controllers (PLCs) in manufacturing systems, providing real-time process variables for Digital Twin synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PLCDataTransmitted
]

instance SCADASystemInterface : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Interfaces with Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems to capture high-frequency operational telemetry from industrial equipment, enabling real-time monitoring in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SCADADataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance IoTSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IoTSensorDataCollector
]

instance PLCDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PLCDataCollector
]

instance SCADADataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SCADASystemInterface
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Aggregates and normalizes heterogeneous data streams from physical sources into structured digital representations for analysis, supporting filtering, normalization, and feature extraction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationMiddleware
]

instance HeterogeneousDataModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Represents the unified digital data structure combining IoT sensor readings, PLC parameters, and SCADA telemetry after preprocessing, enabling cross-domain analysis in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IoTSensorDataTransmitted, PLCDataTransmitted, SCADADataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationMiddleware, SimulationEngine
]

instance HistoricalDataTraceModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stores and manages historical operational data traces for trend analysis, failure prognosis, and performance benchmarking within the Digital Twin ecosystem."
    DTDFVocab:fromData IoTSensorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsModule, AnomalyDetectionEngine
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Defines the system's functional architecture using SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs), decomposing high-level requirements into component-connector structures for Digital Twin implementation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingTool, ArchitectureValidationEngine
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Specifies logical components, interfaces, and interconnections using Architecture Description Languages (ADLs) like AADL or MontiArc, enabling behavioral modeling integration with physical system representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcTool
]

instance PhysicsBasedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements physics-based simulations (e.g., thermal, structural, or fluid dynamics) using MathWorks Simulink or general-purpose programming languages (C++/Java) for accurate system behavior prediction."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine, ControlAlgorithmGenerator
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataAggregationMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Middleware layer (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) responsible for collecting, normalizing, and routing heterogeneous data streams from physical sources to Digital Twin models, ensuring low-latency integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, OptimizationService, PredictionService
]

instance SysMLModelingTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-compatible platform (e.g., IBM Engineering Systems Design Rhapsody) enabling multi-domain modeling across requirements, architecture, and behavior layers for Digital Twin development."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService, ModelValidationService
]

instance MontiArcTool : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component-based modeling framework supporting MontiArc Automaton for behavioral specification and inter-component communication in Digital Twin architectures."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink or equivalent simulation environment executing dynamic system models, control algorithms, and co-simulation setups for Digital Twin validation and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimizationService, PredictionService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsModule : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML-based module leveraging historical and real-time data traces to forecast system degradation, failures, or performance trends using physics-based or machine learning models."
    DTDFVocab:enables PrognosticService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance AnomalyDetectionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Pattern recognition system analyzing aggregated data streams to detect deviations from expected behavior, triggering alerts for maintenance or operational adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance ControlAlgorithmGenerator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Generates and executes closed-loop control algorithms (e.g., PID, MPC) within the Digital Twin, producing autonomous intervention commands for physical system actuators."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlService
]

instance ArchitectureValidationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Validates architectural models against system requirements and behavioral specifications, ensuring consistency across functional, logical, and physical layers in Digital Twin development."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides real-time state synchronization and bidirectional data flow between physical and digital domains, enabling active interpretation of system states for operational awareness."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight, ContextualDataInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes simulation-driven optimization to propose adjustments to physical system parameters, acting as a virtual testbed for control strategy validation before deployment."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction, ControlStrategyValidationInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Leverages prognostic modeling to forecast system degradation, failures, or performance trends using historical and real-time data traces embedded in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePrognosisInsight, PerformanceTrendInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Generates autonomous control signals for physical system actuators based on Digital Twin-derived insights, enabling adaptive reconfiguration of system logic."
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuatorControlAction, SystemReconfigurationAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers multi-perspective dashboards integrating 3D models, real-time telemetry, and simulation outputs for stakeholders including engineers, operators, and managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides SituationalAwarenessInsight, WhatIfAnalysisInsight
]

instance PrognosticService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides long-term system health predictions by analyzing aggregated data traces, supporting predictive maintenance scheduling and resource planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceScheduleAction, ResourceAllocationInsight
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Detects deviations from expected behavior in real-time data streams, alerting stakeholders to potential system malfunctions or inefficiencies."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertAction, RootCauseInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Validates Digital Twin models against physical system behavior through simulation and testing, ensuring fidelity and consistency across abstraction layers."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight, ValidationReportAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of physical system states (e.g., sensor data, operational parameters) synchronized in real-time with the Digital Twin representation."
]

]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

]

]

instance PerformanceTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

]

instance SystemReconfigurationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

]

instance SituationalAwarenessInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational awareness for stakeholders, integrating 3D models, telemetry, and simulation outputs for informed decision-making."
]

instance WhatIfAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

instance MaintenanceScheduleAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

]

]

    base:desc "Validation results comparing Digital Twin model predictions with actual physical system behavior, ensuring model fidelity."

]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system’s orchestration and coordination are implicitly tied to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which emphasize **multi-perspective integration** of heterogeneous models (e.g., SysML for architecture, Simulink/MathWorks for behavior, or C++/Java for implementation). The **architecture patterns** leverage **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., ACME, AADL, or MontiArc) to define **interfaces, interconnections, and composition hierarchies**. **Resource management** is not explicitly detailed, but the use of **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) and **general-purpose languages** (C++, Java) suggests dynamic allocation of computational resources for real-time or near-real-time processing. **System-wide coordination** would rely on **ADLs with behavioral extensions** (e.g., MontiArc Automaton or SysML state machines) to synchronize state transitions across components. The document does not specify orchestration frameworks (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or real-time protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system incorporates **fidelity requirements** aligned with MBSE methodologies, ensuring models accurately represent the physical system’s structure and behavior across abstraction layers. **Validation and verification (V&V)** methods are implied through the use of **SysML state machines, MontiArc Automaton, and simulation tools (e.g., Simulink)**, which enable formal behavioral validation. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between **model abstractions (e.g., logical vs. physical layers)** and **data aggregation processes**, particularly in heterogeneous toolchains. While explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quantitative uncertainty quantification** are not detailed, the integration of **active components** (e.g., simulation tools) suggests dynamic adjustments to maintain model validity. **Quality assurance** is inherently tied to MBSE practices, such as traceability across requirements, functional, and technical layers, though specific protocols (e.g., ISO standards) are not referenced."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is implied to support **heterogeneous communication protocols** and **network topologies** to accommodate the integration of diverse modeling tools and data sources. While the document does not specify exact protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or HTTP/REST), it references the need for **interoperability** between tools like SysML, Simulink [28], and general-purpose languages (e.g., C++/Java). The infrastructure must handle **bandwidth and latency requirements** dictated by real-time or near-real-time data flows, particularly for **event-triggered updates** from physical systems. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the system’s reliance on **active components** (e.g., for data interpretation in DTs [35]–[37]) suggests fault tolerance is critical. **Security protocols** for network communication are also implied but unspecified; however, the integration of heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives [31] necessitates robust authentication and authorization frameworks to ensure data integrity and confidentiality."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for a Digital Twin (DT) system is not explicitly detailed in the provided document. While the document discusses **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and the use of tools like SysML, Simulink [28], and general-purpose programming languages (e.g., C++/Java), it does not specify **hosting platforms** (e.g., cloud, edge, or on-premise), **computational/storage requirements**, or **scalability mechanisms**. The document also does not address **deployment models** (e.g., containerization, microservices, or hybrid architectures) or **availability/reliability assurance** strategies. Thus, the technical specifics of deployment remain unspecified."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document highlights **horizontal integration** through **heterogeneous tool and model interoperability**, particularly in MBSE contexts. **Data exchange protocols** are not explicitly named, but the integration of **SysML (for architecture), Simulink/MathWorks (for simulation), and general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** implies reliance on **standardized modeling notations** (e.g., SysML’s UML-based profiles) and **tool-specific APIs** (e.g., MathWorks’ Simulink Coder for code generation). **Interoperability standards** are inferred from the use of **ADLs (e.g., AADL, MontiArc)** and **SysML IBDs**, which support cross-tool compatibility via **model transformation** or **shared metadata schemas**. Dependencies on **external systems** (e.g., enterprise ERP/MES) are not detailed, but the **digital shadow reference model** (Fig. 1) suggests integration with **manufacturing-specific data sources** (e.g., sensors, PLCs) via **one-way data flows**. **Integration patterns** with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems are not specified, though the **multi-stakeholder perspective** in MBSE implies **service-oriented or event-driven architectures** for loose coupling."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates **safety-critical system considerations** through the **safety cell**, which restricts the maximum working range of the first vertical axis to **[-90°, 90°]** and enforces operational boundaries to prevent collisions or unsafe conditions. **Cybersecurity and access control** are implicitly addressed via the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller**, which manages **authenticated access** to control inputs (position, speed, acceleration) and outputs (target/actual position, derivatives, motor current). The system employs **redundant sensor validation** via **two position encoders per drive** (motor encoder and secondary encoder post-gearbox) to mitigate **backlash and positional uncertainty**, enabling real-time **friction and elasticity compensation** in the drive train. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are inferred through the **gear transmission and cycloidal gear design**, which ensure torque transmission reliability under operational constraints (e.g., maximum speed of **75°/s**). **Risk assessment** is partially addressed by the **closed-loop control architecture** of the SINUMERIK 840D, which monitors motor current (*I_M*) and torque (*τ_M*, *τ_G*, *τ_out*) to prevent overload conditions. However, explicit **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection) or **data protection regulations compliance** (e.g., GDPR, ISO 27001) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]
